class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_dim, hidden_dim):
        super().__init__()
        self.weight = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.Tensor(input_dim, hidden_dim))
        self.bias = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.Tensor(hidden_dim))
        self.reset_parameters()

    def reset_parameters(self):
        torch.nn.init.normal_(self.weight, 0, 1)
        torch.nn.init.zeros_(self.bias)

        x = torch.mm(x, self.weight)
        x = torch.ge(x, self.bias)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_channels, output_channels):
        super(Model, self).__init__()
        # The GLU (Gated Linear Unit) layer is a simple gated version of a linear layer
        # It applies a softmax along the channel dimension (dimension 1) to the input
        self.glu = nn.GLU(dim=1)
        # The softmax function applied in the forward pass ensures that the output probabilities sum up to 1
        self.softmax = F.softmax

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.glu(x)
        x = self.softmax(x, dim=1)
        return x

    # Create a batch of random input tensors with the specified channel dimension
    return [torch.randn(64, 8, 128, 128)]  # batch size 64, 8 channels, 128x128 spatial dimensions

import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, grid_shape_x, grid_shape_y, softplus_threshold):
        super().__init__()
        self.softplus_threshold = softplus_threshold

    def forward(self, x):
        grid_x, grid_y = torch.meshgrid(
            torch.arange(x.shape[-2]).to(x.device), torch.arange(x.shape[-1]).to(x.device), indexing="ij"
        )
        x = x + grid_x + grid_y
        x = F.softplus(x, threshold=self.softplus_threshold)
        return x

import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_tensor_dim, comparison_value, exponent):
        super().__init__()
        self.input_tensor_dim = input_tensor_dim
        self.comparison_value = comparison_value
        self.exponent = exponent

        comparison_result = torch.gt(x, self.comparison_value)
        powered_result = torch.pow(x, self.exponent)
        result = torch.where(comparison_result, powered_result, x)

import torch


class Model(torch.nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, split_size, split_dim, diag_dim):
        super().__init__()
        self.split_size = split_size
        self.split_dim = split_dim
        self.diag_dim = diag_dim

    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        # Split the tensor along the specified dimension
        x_split = torch.split(x, self.split_size, dim=self.split_dim)

        # Create a diagonal matrix for each split tensor
        diagonal_tensors = [torch.diag(split) for split in x_split]

        # Concatenate the diagonal matrices along a new dimension
        x = torch.cat(diagonal_tensors, dim=-1)

        return x

class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, einsum_equation, num_channels, num_groups):
        super().__init__()
        self.einsum_equation = einsum_equation
        self.group_norm = nn.GroupNorm(num_groups, num_channels)

    def forward(self, x):
        # Reshape input to use in einsum
        batch_size, num_channels, height, width = x.shape
        x = x.view(batch_size, num_channels, -1)

        # Apply einsum operation
        x = torch.einsum(self.einsum_equation, x)

        # Reshape back to original dimensions
        x = x.view(batch_size, num_channels, height, width)

        # Apply GroupNorm
        x = self.group_norm(x)

        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, emb_size, vocab_size, hardswish_scale):
        super().__init__()
        self.embedding = nn.Embedding(vocab_size, emb_size)
        self.hardswish = nn.Hardswish()
        self.hardswish_scale = hardswish_scale

    def forward(self, input_mat, element_values):
        emb_output = self.embedding(input_mat)
        mask = torch.isin(input_mat, element_values)
        emb_output_masked = emb_output * mask.unsqueeze(-1).expand_as(emb_output)
        output = self.hardswish(emb_output_masked * self.hardswish_scale)
        return output

        super().__init__()
        self.in_channels = in_channels
        self.out_channels = out_channels

        self.stride = stride
        self.padding = padding

        )
        return F.conv1d(x, weight, self.bias, stride=self.stride, padding=self.padding, dilation=self.dilation)

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, input_dim, clip_value, log_softmax_dim):
        super().__init__()
        self.clip_value = clip_value
        self.log_softmax_dim = log_softmax_dim

    def forward(self, x):
        x = torch.clip(x, max=self.clip_value)
        x = F.log_softmax(x, dim=self.log_softmax_dim)
        return x

import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class Model(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_features, out_features, bias_shape):
        super().__init__()
        self.linear = nn.Linear(in_features, out_features)
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.linear(x)
        x = torch.logical_or(x > 0, self.bias > 0)
        return x

bias_shape = (out_features,)

"""

# Load the CUDA extension
fused_ops = load_inline(
    name='fused_ops',
    cpp_sources=cpp_src,

class ModelNew(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_features, out_features, bias_shape):
        super().__init__()
        self.linear = nn.Linear(in_features, out_features)
        self.bias = nn.Parameter(torch.randn(bias_shape))

    def forward(self, x):
        x = fused_ops.linear_cuda(x, self.linear.weight, self.linear.bias)
        x = fused_ops.logical_or_cuda(x, self.bias)
